entity.summary: New Zealand-associated Namecoin and Bitcoin exchange operated under the Bitparking name by Chris Double. After an exchange data-drive corruption incident, Bitparking disabled trading and announced on April 5, 2013 that the exchange would close after a short withdrawal window.
entity.notes: HEI now models Bitparking as dead / voluntary_shutdown because a contemporaneous forum copy of the official exchange notice explicitly states that trading was disabled and the exchange would close on 2013-04-05 after withdrawals. The underlying evidence is community-preserved rather than a currently accessible first-party page, so confidence remains medium.

expected.summary: Namecoin / Bitcoin exchange operated under the Bitparking name and associated with Chris Double. Public forum records show exchange.bitparking.com operating by 2012, followed by an April 2013 exchange data-drive corruption incident, withdrawal-only recovery efforts, and statements that the exchange would cease operation.
expected.notes: HEI models Bitparking as inactive rather than dead because available sources strongly support exchange operation, drive-corruption failure, withdrawal-only recovery, and cessation language, but the current evidence set is largely forum / community-preserved rather than a clean official shutdown notice. Do not assign a death_reason without stronger terminal evidence.

changes.summary: New Zealand-associated Namecoin and Bitcoin exchange operated under the Bitparking name by Chris Double. After an exchange data-drive corruption incident, Bitparking disabled trading and announced on April 5, 2013 that the exchange would close after a short withdrawal window.
changes.notes: HEI now models Bitparking as dead / voluntary_shutdown because a contemporaneous forum copy of the official exchange notice explicitly states that trading was disabled and the exchange would close on 2013-04-05 after withdrawals. The underlying evidence is community-preserved rather than a currently accessible first-party page, so confidence remains medium.

events.title: Bitparking announced that the exchange would close
events.description: A contemporaneous copy of Bitparking's exchange notice stated that trading had been disabled and that the exchange would close after a short withdrawal-only period following a data-drive corruption incident.
events.notes: The notice is preserved through a Bitcointalk discussion quoting the exchange message.

events.title: Bitparking disabled trading and moved to closure
events.description: Bitparking disabled exchange trading on April 5, 2013 and provided only a brief withdrawal window before ending exchange operation.
events.notes: The precise terminal marker follows the dated shutdown notice and contemporaneous post-closure recovery discussion.

evidence.title: Bitparking exchange closing notice preserved on Bitcointalk
evidence.notes: Forum post reproducing the official exchange notice that trading was disabled and Bitparking would close after withdrawals.

evidence.title: Bitparking exchange closing notice preserved on Bitcointalk
evidence.notes: Preserved shutdown notice identifying April 5, 2013 as the trading-disablement and closure date.

evidence.title: Bitparking recovery and withdrawal discussion after closure
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous community-preserved discussion of the data-drive failure, withdrawal recovery, and closure period.
